params.text:
The Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974

This Act is the foundation of workplace health and safety in Great Britain, enforced by the Health and Safety Executive (HSE). It sets out duties for both employers and employees.

What your employer must do

  • Provide a safe workplace, safe equipment and safe systems of work — "so far as is reasonably practicable".
  • Give you the information, instruction, training and supervision you need to work safely.
  • Carry out risk assessments and put sensible controls in place.
  • Provide any personal protective equipment (PPE) free of charge.

What you must do

  • Take reasonable care of your own health and safety and that of anyone affected by what you do (or fail to do).
  • Co-operate with your employer on health and safety — follow procedures and use equipment properly.
  • Never misuse or interfere with anything provided for safety.
  • Report hazards, faults, accidents and near misses.

The Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999 build on the Act and make risk assessment an explicit legal requirement.

params.question: You are asked to use a stand-aid hoist you have never been trained on to move Mr Okafor, who cannot weight-bear. What is the correct action?

answers.text: Decline to use it until you have been trained and follow the person's moving-and-handling plan, explaining why to your senior.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. Both the law and your duty of care mean you should not use equipment you are untrained on. Get trained and follow the care plan.

answers.text: Have a go anyway so Mr Okafor isn't kept waiting.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Using unfamiliar equipment risks serious injury to Mr Okafor and to you. You must be trained first.

answers.text: Move him by hand with a colleague instead, lifting under his arms.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Drag-lifting under the arms is unsafe and can cause shoulder injury and skin damage. Use the correct equipment following training and the care plan.

answers.text: Ignore the plan and use a different hoist you prefer.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: You must follow the person's individual moving-and-handling plan and only use equipment you are competent and authorised to use.
